articles.title: Cops in plain clothes wielding lathis during CJP protest: Necessity or ambiguity?
articles.description: Plainclothes police involvement in protests drew criticism for lacking uniforms and identification. Senior officers explained this is necessary for intelligence gathering and undercover operations. Such deployments are not new and aim to manage large crowds effectively. While not strictly prohibited, it creates ambiguity during public demonstrations. Police leadership now discourages this practice unless situations escalate or manpower is short.
articles.full_text: Plainclothes police involvement in protests drew criticism for lacking uniforms and identification. articles.title: FBI nabs Harmanveer Singh over Canada-based gang's drug trafficking racket
articles.description: An Indian national, Harmanveer Singh, was arrested in California for alleged drug trafficking.  He is linked to a Canada-based organized crime group led by Ravinder Dhanda.  This arrest is part of a larger international law enforcement operation targeting crime syndicates.  Authorities charged thirty-seven individuals under "Operation Hard Ball" across multiple countries.  Another Indian national, Nitish Kaushal, was also arrested while attempting to cross into Canada.

articles.title: Neeraj Chopra, Mirabai Chanu lead India's medal hopes at CWG 2026
articles.description: India begins its Commonwealth Games campaign in Glasgow with a compact contingent.  The program features ten sports, a significant reduction from previous games.  Athletics forms the backbone of the campaign with many competitors.  Mirabai Chanu and Lovlina Borgohain are key figures for India.  Glasgow offers a learning opportunity before India hosts the games in 2030.

articles.title: 'Put your own house in order': India slams Pakistan over crossborder terror at UN
articles.description: India strongly criticized Pakistan's continued use of crossborder terrorism as a state policy.  Ambassador Parvathaneni Harish stated Jammu and Kashmir is an inalienable part of India.  He highlighted Pakistan's illegal occupation as the sole unresolved issue concerning the region.  Crossborder terrorism has eroded the trust needed for bilateral cooperation, India noted.  Pakistan should focus on its domestic challenges instead of targeting India.

articles.title: “We’re told they…”: Candace Owens challenges Andrew Kolvet with ‘retraction’ demand in escalating Charlie Kirk feud
articles.description: Candace Owens and TPUSA spokesman Andrew Kolvet publicly clashed after Kolvet questioned a Daily Mail report involving Erika Kirk. Owens responded by posting screenshots she said showed the newspaper requested comment before publication. Kolvet accused her of benefiting from the controversy, while Owens challenged TPUSA to seek legal action if the reported quotes were fabricated.

articles.title: US, Saudi Arabia sign civil nuclear deal paving way for uranium enrichment
articles.description: The United States and Saudi Arabia signed a civil nuclear cooperation agreement. This pact allows for a joint study on uranium enrichment facilities. US officials stated the deal upholds safety and nonproliferation standards. Congress will review the agreement, facing potential opposition from lawmakers. The deal comes amid ongoing military operations against Iran.

articles.title: Stock market live updates today: What will guide NSE Nifty50, BSE Sensex today?
articles.full_text: Asian markets traded mostly higher, reflecting improved investor sentiment across the region. South Korea's KOSPI emerged as the top performer, surging 2.88%, while Japan's Nikkei 225 gained 0.67%. Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index (HSI) rose 0.7%, supported by buying in heavyweight stocks. In China, Shanghai Composite edged up 0.02%, while the Shenzhen Composite advanced 0.46%. Australia's ASX 200 climbed 0.65%, and Taiwan's benchmark index added 0.14%. Meanwhile, Singapore's STI slipped 0.35% and Malaysia's benchmark eased 0.06%. Overall, gains across major Asian markets outweighed the losses, with investors closely tracking geopolitical developments, commodity prices and broader global market cues. Stock market extended its losing streak in the previous session, with  Sensex and Nifty closing lower for the third consecutive session. The slump came as surging crude oil prices and heavy selling in banking stocks dampened investor sentiment. BSE Sensex dropped 715.06 points, or 0.92%, to settle at 76,755.05 after falling as much as 828.92 points during the session. NSE Nifty declined 191.45 points, or 0.79%, to end below the 24,000 mark at 23,996.25. The sell-off was broad-based, with mid-cap and small-cap indices also ending over 1% lower. Banking heavyweights including ICICI Bank, Axis Bank and State Bank of India were among the biggest drags, while sectors such as realty, PSU banks and IT witnessed sharp declines. FMCG emerged as the only sectoral gainer, reflecting a shift towards defensive stocks. Market sentiment remained under pressure as rising crude oil prices, geopolitical tensions in the Middle East and concerns over global trade kept investors firmly in risk-off mode, even as the earnings season gathered pace. Crude oil prices extended their rally, climbing more than 1.5% to their highest level in over six weeks as escalating geopolitical tensions stoked fresh concerns over global supply disruptions. Brent crude futures rose $1.93, or 2%, to touch $96 per barrel after already surging more than $3 in the previous session to settle at $94.07. The latest gains came after the United States launched a fresh round of strikes on Iran, while Yemen's Houthi rebels reportedly targeted oil tankers in the Red Sea, intensifying worries over the security of key energy shipping routes. Rising crude prices are likely to remain a major concern for equity markets, particularly for oil-importing countries such as India, as higher energy costs could fuel inflation, pressure the rupee and weigh on corporate earnings. Investors will continue to track geopolitical developments closely, with any further escalation likely to keep oil prices volatile and influence sentiment across global financial markets. Rupee's sharp depreciation against the US dollar is expected to remain in focus as currency weakness reflects growing investor caution. The currency slipped to 96.53 against the greenback in the previous session, pressured by rising crude oil prices, geopolitical uncertainty and weakness in equity market. A weaker rupee could increase import costs for businesses, particularly those dependent on overseas raw materials, while offering some support to export-oriented companies. Currency traders are also tracking the dollar index, global risk sentiment and possible RBI intervention. Any further depreciation could influence market sentiment, especially if crude prices continue their upward march. Global developments are likely to remain the primary driver for Dalal Street as investors weigh geopolitical tensions, crude oil prices and currency movements alongside corporate earnings. Asian markets delivered a mixed performance in the previous session, while US equities ended higher, reflecting divergent investor sentiment across regions. Market participants will also monitor developments related to the Middle East, shipping disruptions and trade-related headlines that could influence commodity prices. With domestic macroeconomic data relatively light, overseas cues are expected to play a larger role in shaping investor behaviour until fresh earnings and economic indicators provide stronger direction.

articles.title: Oil prices touch six week high as Strait of Hormuz is 'completely closed'
articles.description: Crude oil prices surged as Middle East conflict escalated on Thursday. Brent crude rose above $96 a barrel and WTI climbed past $88. Fresh attacks on oil tankers in the Red Sea fueled supply concerns. Iran's Revolutionary Guards claimed control of the Strait of Hormuz. Houthi naval blockades further amplified worries over global energy shipments.

articles.title: PSSSB Jail Warder Admit card 2026 released at sssb.punjab.gov.in; candidates can download hall ticket through email and website
articles.description: The Punjab Subordinate Service Selection Board has released the PSSSB Jail Warder Admit Card 2026 for the written examination scheduled on July 26, 2026. Candidates applying for Jail Warder and Matron posts can download the hall ticket through the official website or registered email IDs. The recruitment drive is being conducted for 532 vacancies under Advertisement Number 08/2026. The selection process includes a written examination followed by the Physical Measurement Test.

articles.title: Three Indian engineers spent six months in a library studying quantum computing, now they are building what Google has been chasing
articles.description: Three former semiconductor engineers—Sujoy Chakravarty, Ravi Mehta and Biman Chattopadhyay—built a successful chip IP company, sold it to Synopsys, and launched Bengaluru-based Quanfluence to develop room-temperature quantum computers. After months of studying quantum technologies, they chose photonics as the most practical approach. Supported by academic experts, the startup now offers commercial cloud-based quantum computing solutions, demonstrating India's growing capabilities in frontier technology while highlighting the importance of lifelong learning, engineering excellence and innovation.

articles.title: TN Supplementary result 2026 declared for private candidates at dge.tn.gov.in: Direct link to download scorecards
articles.description: The Directorate of Government Examinations (DGE), Tamil Nadu, has announced the TN Supplementary Result 2026 for private candidates of Classes 10, 11 and 12 arrear examinations. Students can download their provisional marksheets from the official portal using their roll number and date of birth. The online scorecard contains essential academic details required for admissions and future academic processes.

articles.title: Delhi University Political Science department turns 75, honours former faculty and launches academic journal
articles.description: The University of Delhi's Department of Political Science marked its Diamond Jubilee with a programme featuring the launch of a new academic journal, addresses by university leaders and invited speakers, and a felicitation ceremony for former faculty members and alumni. Speakers reflected on the department's academic journey, higher education and knowledge systems. The event concluded with a cultural performance by a delegation from the Ministry of Culture, Government of NCT of Delhi.

articles.title: 'Jana Nayagan' Movie Review and Release Live Updates
articles.full_text: The festive atmosphere began several hours before the first screenings. Fans gathered outside theatres in large numbers to welcome the release of their favourite star's farewell film. Sharing his experience of working with the actor, KVN said, "Working with Vijay sir is a great learning experience. His dedication, discipline and determination are unbelievable." ​ One of the biggest talking points surrounding Jana Nayagan has been its reported HD leak months before release. Despite piracy concerns, fans have continued to book tickets in huge numbers. The advance booking suggests that audiences still prefer watching Vijay's final film on the big screen. Tamil Nadu has emerged as the biggest market for the film. The state alone contributed around Rs 18-19 crore in Day 1 advance bookings. The response once again highlights Vijay's unmatched popularity among Tamil audiences. Out of the total advance booking, nearly Rs 30 crore has come from the film's first day. Such figures underline Vijay's massive box office pull, even after a lengthy release delay.

articles.title: Naseeruddin SLAMS Bollywood's silence on CJP protests
articles.description: Naseeruddin Shah criticised Bollywood's silence on the Cockroach Janta Party protests, comparing silent actors to "a dog with a bone" that can't bark. He shared a video supporting protesting students, saying "sab yaad rakha jayega." While many actors stayed quiet, Shabana Azmi and Ritesh Deshmukh voiced their support for the protests.
